metadata.name: AntiVEGF-Guideline-KG
metadata.description: Clinically-ordered 4-layer KG: Visual -> Pathophysiology -> DECISION -> RESPONSE. v0.3 FLIPS the v0.2 chain so the causal order matches practice: the clinician first DECIDES whether to (continue) anti-VEGF from the current image + pre-treatment metadata (guideline-based), and the treatment RESPONSE (anatomic + functional + fluid) is the downstream OUTCOME predicted given that decision -- not a prior that drives the decision. Diagnosis is NO LONGER given in the input: pathophysiology is inferred from the image so Step 2 is a genuine inference, not a relabel of a supplied diagnosis.
metadata.method_source: Wang MH, Sensors 2025 25:6879 (neuro-symbolic causal/temporal reasoning + counterfactual). v0.3 corrects the temporal direction: DECISION is the guideline node (image+metadata -> continue/stop); RESPONSE is the conditional outcome node (decision -> expected delta-CST + delta-VA + fluid resolution -> responder/non-responder).
metadata.design_note: Input = PRE-treatment image ONLY (diagnosis removed; metadata optionally injected for the metadata ablation). Step 3 DECISION is supervised by the recorded continue/stop label. Step 4 RESPONSE is supervised by the MEASURED composite outcome (delta-CST bucket + delta-VA direction + pre->post fluid resolution). Single-image limit finding now lives cleanly on the terminal RESPONSE node; the DECISION node is a separate guideline classification.

nodes.label: Active exudative lesion
nodes.narrative: an active exudative lesion (intra-/subretinal fluid leakage from an active neovascular or edematous process)

nodes.label: PED without active fluid
nodes.narrative: a pigment epithelial detachment without active intra- or sub-retinal fluid, an activity-indeterminate finding

nodes.label: Quiescent / dry macula
nodes.narrative: a quiescent dry macula with no active exudation

nodes.label: Good treatment responder
nodes.narrative: a good treatment response (meaningful CST reduction and/or visual-acuity gain)

nodes.label: Poor / non-responder
nodes.narrative: a poor anatomic-functional response (little CST reduction and no visual gain) despite anti-VEGF

nodes.label: No active disease to treat
nodes.narrative: no active disease for anti-VEGF to act on; the macula is expected to remain anatomically stable

decision_guideline_rules.rationale: Subretinal fluid -> active exudation -> guideline favors continuation.
Intraretinal fluid -> active edema -> guideline favors continuation.
No active fluid (dry macula) -> guideline favors stopping.
PED only, no active fluid -> case-dependent (metadata-modulated).

decision_guideline_rules.note: When metadata is provided (metadata ablation), these modulate the guideline decision; in the base setting only the image findings drive Step 3.

response_definition.note: Step-4 RESPONSE is a COMPOSITE of three measured outcomes; the neovascular/edematous response is NOT judged by CST alone.
response_definition.composite_responder_rule: good_responder if (delta_cst <= -25  OR  delta_va >= +0.1); else poor_responder. Fluid resolution reported as supporting evidence (rare: ~5% of fluid eyes resolve in this cohort).
